title: ElevenLabs API Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers to effectively use the ElevenLabs API for AI-driven tasks.

commands.description: Generate text using the ElevenLabs API.
commands.scenario: When you need to generate creative content or responses based on input prompts.

commands.description: Set the randomness of the output for more creative responses.
commands.scenario: When generating marketing copy that requires creative variations.

commands.description: Limit the number of tokens in the generated response.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers for a chatbot response.

commands.description: Specify the model version for generation tasks.
commands.scenario: When you require a specific model version for compatibility reasons.

commands.description: Enable streaming of responses for real-time applications.
commands.scenario: When building a live chat application that requires immediate feedback.

commands.command: prompt='Translate the following text to French: {text}'
commands.description: Use prompt engineering for translation tasks.
commands.scenario: When integrating a translation feature into an application.

commands.description: Reduce repetition in generated output.
commands.scenario: When generating long-form content that should avoid repetitive phrases.

commands.description: Encourage the model to introduce new topics.
commands.scenario: When brainstorming ideas and need diverse suggestions.

commands.description: Generate speech from text input using the ElevenLabs API.
commands.scenario: When converting text to speech for accessibility features.

commands.description: Select a specific voice for text-to-speech conversion.
commands.scenario: When you need a specific voice profile for branding purposes.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Prompt technique to improve reasoning in responses.
commands.scenario: When solving complex problems that require logical reasoning.

commands.description: Enable detailed logging for troubleshooting.
commands.scenario: When diagnosing issues with API requests or responses.

commands.description: Set the context length for the model to consider in responses.
commands.scenario: When handling large documents that require extensive context.

commands.description: Set a timeout for API requests to prevent hanging.
commands.scenario: When ensuring API calls do not exceed a certain duration in production.

commands.description: Use the streaming endpoint with chunked transfer encoding to reduce Time To First Byte (TTFB) for real-time conversational applications.
Fine-tune the output variance: lower stability increases emotional range/expressiveness, while higher similarity boost ensures the output stays closer to the original voice sample.
Programmatically upload high-quality audio samples to create custom Instant Voice Clones via API.
Engineer prosody and pacing by using specific punctuation patterns or phonetic spelling to force natural breathing pauses and emphasis in the TTS engine.
Monitor real-time character usage and remaining credit balance to implement client-side circuit breakers and prevent API errors.
Use the latency-optimized endpoint for real-time conversational agents to reduce Time To First Byte (TTFB).
Set 'latency_optimization' parameter to 'true' in the request body to prioritize speed over high-fidelity nuances for real-time use cases.
Retrieve a list of previous generation requests to audit costs, re-download assets, or debug failed synthesis attempts.
Utilize the 'description' field during voice cloning to provide semantic context, helping the model better understand the target persona's characteristics.
Inject emotional cues using bracketed descriptors like '[whispering]', '[shouting]', or '[laughing]' at the start of sentences to influence prosody in certain models.
